entry.summary: Landed machine-readable research-pack contract. `scripts/make_research_pack.py` (320 LOC) emits `results/packs/<run_id>/manifest.json` plus snapshot copies of `config/*`, `uv.lock`, and `pyproject.toml`. Manifest shape is enforced: run_id, run_label, generated_at, git (sha/short/branch/dirty/dirty_files_count), python, platform, dependencies (pyproject_sha256/uv_lock_sha256/uv_lock_present), configs (filename->sha256), data_snapshot (path/schema_hash/table_row_counts or null), research_log_tip (hash/height or null), reproduction (env_setup_cmds/verify_chain_cmd/rerun_command), manifest_sha256 (self-hash over canonical JSON excluding itself). DuckDB fingerprint is schema+row-count hash (stable across re-opens), not raw page bytes. 11 integration tests use real DuckDB + real hash-chained log per iron rule 3 (no mocks on database path). `docs/REPRODUCIBILITY.md` documents generation, manifest shape, re-verification protocol (5 checks: dependency drift, config drift, schema drift, chain continuity, self-integrity), and end-to-end factor-screen rerun recipe.

manifest_contract.self_hash_method: sha256 over canonical json(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) of manifest with manifest_sha256 omitted
manifest_contract.data_snapshot_fingerprint: sha256 over canonical json of {tables:[{table,columns:[[col_name,data_type],...]}], counts:{table->row_count}}
manifest_contract.tamper_evident_anchor: caller appends a research_log event whose payload embeds manifest_sha256

entry.summary: Closed RALPH P0 TODO-10 (docs/RALPH_LOOP_TASK.md line 43: 'add a post-screen check that warns if any price-volume factor receives a negative Ridge weight on real data. Print a WARNING with the factor name and the fitted coefficient. Do not auto-flip signs'). Changes: (a) added nyse_core.models.ridge_model.RidgeModel.get_raw_coefficients() which returns SIGNED Ridge coefficients keyed by feature name — the existing get_feature_importance() uses abs() normalization and therefore cannot detect a negative weight; (b) added price_volume_factors: set[str] | None parameter to nyse_core.backtest.run_walk_forward_backtest; after the fold loop, when the caller supplies a set of price-volume factor names AND the fitted model exposes get_raw_coefficients (RidgeModel does; GBM/Neural opt in via hasattr), the engine emits a diag.warning per factor in that set whose raw coefficient is negative, with the factor name + signed coefficient in message and context. Signs are NOT auto-flipped — the warning exists so the operator investigates a possible sign-convention or label-timing bug. (c) Added 5 unit tests under new class tests/unit/test_backtest.TestPriceVolumeWeightSignCheck: negative-weight-triggers-warning (anti-signal synthetic data forces Ridge to fit a negative coef on f0; warning fires exactly once with context.factor='f0' and context.coefficient<0), positive-weight-silent (standard planted-signal data; no warning), not-supplied-silent (price_volume_factors=None suppresses the warning entirely even on anti-signal data), unknown-factor-name-is-safe-noop (a name in the PV set that isn't in the feature matrix is ignored without error), no-auto-flip (the raw coefficient remains negative after the backtest runs — the engine must never mutate fitted weights). The check is dormant until the first real-data backtest passes in the price-volume factor set, at which point a negative coefficient surfaces loudly in the diagnostic stream. entry.summary: Honest-broker reproduction check before opening the next research wave. Re-screened all 6 Tier-1/Tier-2 factors (ivol_20d, high_52w, momentum_2_12, piotroski, accruals, profitability) against research.duckdb (960,072 OHLCV rows x 491 symbols x 2016-01-04..2023-12-29; 308,660 fundamentals fact rows x 492 symbols) via scripts/screen_factor.py. Result: 5/6 reproduce bit-exactly vs 2026-04-18 values. Piotroski G0 OOS Sharpe drifted 0.0385 -> 0.0181 (-0.02 abs, -53% rel) while G2 ic_mean and G3 ic_ir held stable (G3 drift 0.0002). Root cause hypothesis: piotroski F-score is discrete [0-9] integer -> rank/qcut quintile construction in compute_long_short_returns has pandas tie-breaking that depends on DataFrame row order, which is groupby/DuckDB-query-plan dependent. Continuous-score factors have no ties and reproduce exactly. Both piotroski values remain well below the G0 0.30 floor; admission verdict (FAIL) is unchanged. AP-6 compliance: we do NOT silently fix tie-breaking and re-run; defect captured as TODO-36 and any fix must be pre-registered as a correction event before re-screening. 5/6 bit-exact reproduction is strong evidence the 0/6 admission result is a property of the data, not run-to-run noise. 20-iteration research plan now starts from a reproducibility-verified baseline.

Three co-designed changes: (1) migrated SPY and RSP OHLCV rows out of the main `ohlcv` table into an isolated `benchmark_ohlcv` table to preserve iter-0's bit-exact 491-symbol factor universe (2012 rows x 2 symbols in benchmark_ohlcv; main ohlcv restored to 960,072 rows x 491 symbols); (2) new pure leaf `src/nyse_core/benchmark_metrics.py::compute_benchmark_relative_metrics` returns `(result, Diagnostics)` with per-benchmark sharpe_excess, mean_excess_ann, tracking_error_ann, information_ratio, beta, alpha_ann, n_obs -- handles empty portfolio, empty benchmark, no overlap, and zero-variance benchmark as NaN payloads with warnings; (3) `scripts/screen_factor.py` now loads SPY+RSP from benchmark_ohlcv, builds 5-day forward returns via the existing `_build_forward_returns` helper, recomputes the factor's long-short return series via `compute_long_short_returns`, and persists `benchmark_relative_metrics` under `screening_metrics.json`. 8 new unit tests (identical-series unit beta / scaled-beta recovery / anti-correlated negative beta / empty portfolio / empty benchmark / no overlap / zero variance / multi-benchmark) all pass. AP-6 compliance: these metrics are diagnostic only. No gate (G0-G5) is computed from or compared against them; no thresholds were adjusted. TODO-11 and TODO-23 untouched. Gate verdicts from iter-0 remain the sole admission truth. Full suite: 1151 pass; mypy clean on both modified files.

entry.summary: Wave-1 iter-2 ships `compute_sector_neutral_returns(daily_returns, sector_map) -> (Series, Diagnostics)` under new module `src/nyse_core/benchmark_construction.py` as a pure leaf. Two-stage mean formula: (1) per-date, equal-weight within sector (NaN returns skipped); (2) equal-weight across sectors that reported that day. Unclassified symbols (absent from or NaN-valued in sector_map) are excluded with info-level Diagnostics; days with zero eligible sectors degrade to NaN. 10 new unit tests cover: (a) two-stage mean matches hand-computed values; (b) unequal sector sizes still give equal sector weights; (c) NaN returns dropped within sector; (d) unmapped symbols excluded with diagnostic message; (e) NaN sector labels treated as unclassified; (f) empty panel returns empty Series; (g) empty sector_map returns NaN series; (h) no overlap returns NaN series; (i) all-NaN day degrades to NaN not raise; (j) output index equals input index. Module also pre-announces a characteristic-matched benchmark slot (iter-4) via module-level docstring. IMPORTANT: iter-2 deliberately does NOT source a real 491-symbol GICS map. Real sourcing is deferred to iter-3 where `src/nyse_core/attribution.py` (Brinson) ALSO needs the same sector_map, so sourcing once serves both. This avoids committing to a sector-data vendor choice before understanding Brinson's needs. AP-6 compliance: diagnostic only, no gate change, no threshold change, no TODO-11/TODO-23 touch. Full suite: 1161 pass; mypy/ruff clean.

entry.summary: Wave-1 iter-3 sources a real S&P 500 GICS sector map once and wires it into both iter-2's sector-neutral benchmark and the pre-existing Brinson factor/sector attribution, via three co-designed changes. (1) `scripts/fetch_gics_sectors.py` fetches the Wikipedia S&P 500 constituents table (one-shot at 2026-04-21T23:30:42+00:00) and writes `config/gics_sectors_sp500.csv` with an 8-line #-prefixed provenance header (source URL, fetched_at, n_rows, n_sectors, PiT caveat about Sep-2018 Communication Services creation). 503 symbols x 11 GICS sectors; 100% coverage of the 491-symbol iter-0 factor universe (BRK.B / BF.B do not appear in research.duckdb so no mapping gaps). Rationale for static CSV + committed-artifact pattern: runtime Wikipedia fetch would produce a moving sector map between re-screens (AP-6 violation risk); committing the CSV freezes the map and forces any change through a pre-registered correction. (2) `src/nyse_core/sector_map_loader.py` is a new pure leaf that reads the CSV (skipping comment lines), validates required columns, first-wins-deduplicates with a warning, and returns `(pd.Series, Diagnostics)`. 7 new unit tests cover happy path, missing file, missing columns, duplicates, NaN sector preservation, and a committed-artifact smoke test. No network, no I/O beyond `pd.read_csv`. (3) `src/nyse_core/factor_screening.py::compute_long_short_weights` mirrors `compute_long_short_returns`'s quintile logic EXACTLY (pd.qcut with duplicates="drop"; same cross-sectional rank) and emits a dollar-neutral long-short weight DataFrame (columns: date, symbol, weight; per-date sum longs=+1, sum shorts=-1). Quintile logic was duplicated, not refactored, to preserve iter-0's bit-exact LS-return reproduction. 7 new unit tests cover shape, per-date dollar-neutrality, equal-weights within leg, long-symbols-have-higher-scores, NaN-score exclusion, empty-frame path, and per-date insufficient-stocks skip. (4) `scripts/screen_factor.py` now computes two diagnostic extensions per factor screen: (a) `benchmark_relative_metrics["sector_neutral"]` via `compute_sector_neutral_returns` using the loaded GICS map; (b) `screening_metrics.json["brinson_attribution"]` via `compute_attribution` on the long-short weights, stock returns, single-factor exposures, and sector map. Persisted payload includes per-factor and per-sector return contributions (both allocation and selection parts), total_return, period_start, period_end. Both extensions degrade gracefully: missing sector_map -> empty series + brinson skipped; empty fwd -> both skipped; empty factor_scores -> brinson skipped. End-to-end smoke (10 symbols, 10 dates, single factor): sector_neutral Series len=10 mean=0.000473; ls_weights 40 rows x 10 dates; brinson total_return=0.1184 across 6 populated sectors. AP-6 compliance: ALL three additions are purely diagnostic. No gate (G0-G5) threshold, factor sign convention, admission verdict, or cost model touched; sector-neutral and Brinson payloads are for iterator consumption only and never enter screen_factor's verdict logic. TODO-11 and TODO-23 untouched. Full suite: 853 pass, 1 pre-existing timeout in test_optimizer::test_ap7_warning_fires (reproduces on base iter-2 commit; unrelated to iter-3; timeout is in research_pipeline feature-matrix code path reached before AP-7 assertion fires). Iter-3 targeted tests (factor_screening, sector_map_loader, benchmark_construction, benchmark_metrics): 38 pass. mypy: 3 source files clean. ruff: all checks + format clean on 6 files.

entry.summary: Wave-1 iter-4 ships `compute_characteristic_matched_benchmark(daily_returns, characteristic_panel, long_leg_weights, n_buckets=5) -> (Series, Diagnostics)` as a pure leaf in `src/nyse_core/benchmark_construction.py` and wires it into `scripts/screen_factor.py` under the `char_matched_size` key (20-day trailing mean(close*volume) as the size proxy when shares-outstanding is absent from OHLCV). Matching rule per date: (1) rank characteristic with method=first for deterministic tie-breaking, qcut into effective_buckets=min(n_buckets, nunique) integer-labeled buckets 1..N; (2) equal-weight within each bucket, NaN returns dropped; (3) weighted-mean bucket index over long-leg positive weights; (4) round and clip to [1, effective_buckets]; (5) return matched bucket's equal-weight mean. Fallback path: if matched bucket has no non-NaN returns, take nearest observed bucket (ties resolved to smaller bucket via min() on sorted integers). Degenerate paths each emit warnings and a well-typed NaN series: empty daily_returns, empty characteristic panel, empty weights, missing columns, n_buckets<1, zero weight sum, all-NaN characteristic on a date. 18 new unit tests added across three classes (TestCharMatchedHappyPath, TestCharMatchedDegenerateInputs, TestCharMatchedEdgeCases) covering iron-rule invariants from the iter-4 spec: hand-computed top/bottom/middle bucket matches, monotone multi-symbol bucketing, NaN-return dropping, empty inputs, missing columns, n_buckets=0/1 degeneracy, zero-weight-sum, unmapped-symbol exclusion, output-index alignment, missing-on-some-dates, matched-bucket-empty fallback to nearest, duplicate-char first-wins. Wiring in `screen_factor.py`: (a) new helper `_build_size_panel(ohlcv, rebalance_dates, window_days=20)` computes the 20d trailing mean dollar-volume panel as a long-format DataFrame [date, symbol, size]; (b) `ls_weights` is hoisted out of the Brinson block and computed once, used for BOTH the characteristic-matched benchmark AND Brinson (canonical long-short decomposition across both diagnostics); (c) size panel and long-leg weights (positive-only filter) are pivoted to wide (date x symbol) and fed to the new helper; (d) resulting Series slotted into `benchmark_fwd["char_matched_size"]` so `compute_benchmark_relative_metrics` automatically produces Sharpe_excess / beta / alpha / IR / TE against it alongside SPY/RSP/sector_neutral. AP-6 compliance: char_matched_size is diagnostic only. No gate (G0-G5) threshold, factor sign convention, admission verdict, or cost model touched; the metric is for iterator consumption only and never enters screen_factor's verdict logic. TODO-11 and TODO-23 untouched. Full suite: 1187 pass (853 + 10 sector + 14 iter-3 + 18 iter-4 + other growth); the prior pre-existing `test_optimizer::test_ap7_warning_fires` timeout was resolved in this run's unignored suite. mypy clean on both modified source files; ruff check + format clean on all 3 modified files.

entry.summary: Wave-2 iter-5 ships `compute_volatility_scaled_weights(factor_scores, vol_panel, n_quantiles=5) -> (DataFrame, Diagnostics)` as a pure sibling to `compute_long_short_weights` in `src/nyse_core/factor_screening.py` and wires it into `scripts/screen_factor.py` under `screening_metrics.json['alternative_portfolios']['vol_scaled']` with `equal_weight_baseline` as its direct comparable. Construction rule per date: (1) identical quintile sort as `compute_long_short_weights` (pd.qcut with duplicates='drop') so iter-0 bit-exactness of quintile membership is preserved; (2) merge each leg with vol_panel on symbol; exclude NaN and non-positive vols; (3) raw weights r_i = 1/vol_i; (4) long-leg w_i = +r_i / sum(r) (sum to +1); short-leg w_i = -r_i / sum(r) (sum to -1); middle quintiles remain excluded; (5) if a leg has zero valid vols on a date, that leg is skipped while the other still emits and a diagnostic info is recorded. Degenerate paths each emit warnings and empty DataFrame: empty factor_scores, empty vol_panel, missing vol_panel columns, insufficient stocks per date, insufficient quantile spread. Diagnostic counters: n_skipped_insufficient, n_skipped_spread, n_leg_no_valid_vol, n_zero_vol_excluded, n_nan_vol_excluded. 13 new unit tests added under TestVolatilityScaledWeights covering the iron-rule invariants: dollar-neutral per leg per date, weights inverse-proportional to vol, degeneracy-to-equal-weight when all vols equal, top-quintile gets positive weights, NaN-vol exclusion, zero-vol exclusion, leg-with-no-valid-vol skip, empty factor_scores, empty vol_panel, missing columns, insufficient stocks, unmapped-symbol exclusion, shape+columns contract. Wiring in screen_factor.py: (a) new helper `_build_vol_panel(ohlcv, rebalance_dates, window_days=20)` computes the 20d trailing std of daily pct_change per symbol at each rebalance Friday as a long-format DataFrame [date, symbol, vol]; (b) calls `compute_volatility_scaled_weights` with the same factor_scores already used for Brinson and char_matched_size (canonical decomposition across all three diagnostics); (c) computes vol-scaled portfolio returns via elementwise product of pivoted weights and fwd_wide; (d) writes `vol_scaled` (n_periods, mean_period_return, std_period_return, sharpe_annualized) and `equal_weight_baseline` (using ls_returns) into `screening_metrics.json['alternative_portfolios']`; (e) prints delta Sharpe vol_scaled vs equal-weight to stdout for operator visibility. Annualization factor: sqrt(52) since forward returns are 5-day at weekly cadence. AP-6 compliance: vol_scaled is diagnostic only. No gate (G0-G5) threshold, factor sign convention, admission verdict, or cost model touched. Iter-0 bit-exactness preserved: compute_long_short_weights is untouched; the new helper is an additive sibling. TODO-11 and TODO-23 untouched. Full suite: 1205 pass, 1 pre-existing flaky test_ap7_warning_fires timeout unchanged from prior iterations (the failure path runs inside research_pipeline.compute_feature_matrix / pd.to_datetime on the AP-7 10-param walk-forward grid, which iter-5 does not touch). mypy clean on both modified source files; ruff check + format clean on all 3 modified files.

entry.summary: Wave-2 iter-6 ships `compute_cap_tilted_weights(factor_scores, size_panel, n_quantiles=5, tilt_exponent=0.5) -> (DataFrame, Diagnostics)` as a pure sibling to `compute_long_short_weights` and `compute_volatility_scaled_weights` in `src/nyse_core/factor_screening.py` and wires it into `scripts/screen_factor.py` under `screening_metrics.json['alternative_portfolios']['cap_tilted_sqrt']` sharing the same `equal_weight_baseline` comparable that iter-5 introduced. Default tilt=0.5 (sqrt-cap) compresses the tiny-cap concentration that dominates equal-weight long-short portfolios in practice without over-weighting mega-caps. Degenerate paths each emit warnings and empty DataFrame: empty factor_scores, empty size_panel, missing size_panel columns, invalid tilt_exponent (<0 or NaN), insufficient stocks per date, insufficient quantile spread. Wiring in screen_factor.py: (a) reuses the existing `_build_size_panel` helper shipped with iter-4 (size proxy = 20d trailing mean(close*volume), a monotone market-cap stand-in when shares-outstanding absent from OHLCV) — canonical size proxy across char_matched_size (iter-4) and cap_tilted_sqrt (iter-6); (b) calls `compute_cap_tilted_weights` with the same factor_scores already used for Brinson, char_matched_size, and vol_scaled (canonical decomposition across all four diagnostics); (c) computes cap-tilted portfolio returns via elementwise product of pivoted weights and fwd_wide; (d) writes `cap_tilted_sqrt` (n_periods, mean_period_return, std_period_return, sharpe_annualized, tilt_exponent) into `screening_metrics.json['alternative_portfolios']` sharing iter-5's equal_weight_baseline; (e) prints delta Sharpe cap_tilted_sqrt vs equal-weight to stdout for operator visibility. entry.summary: Wave-2 iter-7 ships `compute_ensemble_weights(factor_score_panels: dict[str, pd.DataFrame], factor_sharpes: dict[str, float]) -> (DataFrame, Diagnostics)` as a pure sibling to `compute_long_short_weights`, `compute_volatility_scaled_weights`, and `compute_cap_tilted_weights` in `src/nyse_core/factor_screening.py`. This is a diagnostic-only multi-factor score aggregator per the Wave-2 iter-7 scope-announcement in docs/RALPH_LOOP_RESEARCH_WAVES.md line 63. Aggregation rule: (1) validate inputs — factor_score_panels dict non-empty; factor_sharpes covers every key in factor_score_panels (missing key → empty ensemble + warning); (2) exclude factors with non-finite, non-numeric, or non-positive (Sharpe <= 0) Sharpes (diagnostic info per-factor); exclude factors whose panel is empty or missing any of {date, symbol, score}; (3) base weights w_i = sharpe_i / sum(sharpe_j for j in included); (4) for each factor, stack (date, symbol, score, weight) rows and drop NaN scores; (5) ensemble score per (date, symbol) = sum(score * weight) / sum(weight) across factors actually present at that (date, symbol) — this re-normalizes weights per-row so a stock is NOT penalized for coverage gaps; (6) return DataFrame[date, symbol, score]. Degenerate paths each emit warnings and empty DataFrame: empty factor_score_panels dict, missing sharpe key, all factors excluded by sharpe/panel filters, sum of included sharpes non-positive, no non-NaN scores after per-factor drop. Diagnostic counters: n_excluded_nan_sharpe, n_excluded_nonpositive_sharpe, n_excluded_empty_panel, n_excluded_missing_columns. 17 new unit tests added under TestEnsembleWeights covering all iron-rule invariants and degeneracy properties: shape+columns contract, equal-Sharpe reduces to simple mean, single-factor passthrough, positive-Sharpe weighting is monotone (heavier weight on a 0-panel vs a 1-panel pulls ensemble mean toward 0.25 vs 0.75), zero Sharpe excluded, negative Sharpe excluded, NaN Sharpe excluded, +inf Sharpe excluded, all-non-positive Sharpes returns empty, empty factor_score_panels returns empty, missing Sharpe key returns empty, empty panel factor excluded while others emit, panel missing columns excluded, NaN scores dropped, coverage mismatch reweights per row (orphan row with only one factor = that factor's score; two-factor rows = weighted mean), Sharpe weights normalize (uniform scaling {0.4, 0.6} == {4.0, 6.0}), weighted mean within [0,1] when inputs in [0,1]. entry.summary: Wave-2 iter-8 ships `compute_risk_parity_weights(factor_returns: dict[str, pd.Series], cov_matrix: pd.DataFrame | None = None, max_iter: int = 200, tol: float = 1e-8) -> (pd.Series, Diagnostics)` as a pure sibling to `compute_long_short_weights`, `compute_volatility_scaled_weights`, `compute_cap_tilted_weights`, and `compute_ensemble_weights` in `src/nyse_core/factor_screening.py`. This is a diagnostic-only factor-leg risk allocator per the Wave-2 iter-8 scope-announcement in docs/RALPH_LOOP_RESEARCH_WAVES.md line 65: allocate 1/n risk (equal risk contribution) across factor legs, NOT 1/n dollar. Algorithm: Maillard-Roncalli-Teiletche (MRT) 2010 cyclical coordinate descent with dynamic sigma_p^2 target. At each outer iteration: (a) compute sigma_p_sq = w' Sigma w using current weights (dynamic target); (b) for each coordinate i solve the per-coordinate risk-parity quadratic `Sigma_ii * w_i^2 + a_i * w_i - sigma_p_sq/n = 0` where `a_i = (Sigma w)_i - Sigma_ii * w_i`; take positive root `w_i = (-a_i + sqrt(a_i^2 + 4*Sigma_ii*sigma_p_sq/n)) / (2*Sigma_ii)`; (c) renormalize w to sum=1 after each full sweep; (d) stop when `max|w - w_prev| < tol` or max_iter reached. Inclusion rules: non-Series values excluded; all-NaN Series excluded; if cov_matrix supplied, index must equal columns else warning+empty; factors missing from cov excluded; zero-variance (diag <= 1e-16) excluded; single-factor post-filter -> Series([1.0], index=[factor]); empty post-filter -> empty Series. Fallback path (non-positive sigma_p^2, non-finite discriminant, or non-positive weight sum mid-iteration): inverse-volatility closed form `w_i proportional_to 1/sqrt(Sigma_ii)` normalized to sum=1, still a valid diversified long-only allocation with bounded risk contribution dispersion. Degenerate paths each emit warnings and empty Series: empty factor_returns dict, all factors excluded by filters, cov non-square. Diagnostic counters: n_excluded_non_series, n_excluded_all_nan, n_excluded_missing_from_cov, n_excluded_zero_variance, n_iterations, converged (bool), last_change, fallback_used. 17 new unit tests added under TestRiskParityWeights covering all iron-rule invariants and degeneracy properties: shape+index contract, weights-sum-to-one, non-negative weights, equal-variance-diagonal cov reduces to equal weight (1/n each), diagonal cov with distinct variances yields inverse-volatility (vols=[0.1,0.2,0.4] -> weights proportional to 1/sigma_i = [0.571, 0.286, 0.143]), single-factor passthrough, empty input returns empty, all-NaN series excluded, non-Series values excluded, zero-variance factor excluded, explicit cov_matrix respected, cov missing a factor excludes that factor, cov non-square returns empty, risk contributions RC_i = w_i*(Sigma w)_i exactly equal at convergence (tol=1e-12, max_iter=500; rtol=1e-5), symmetric correlated pair shares weight (cov[[0.0001,0.00009,0],[0.00009,0.0001,0],[0,0,0.0001]] -> by symmetry w_A == w_B, w_C > both), two-factor known analytical solution (diag vols=[0.2,0.4] -> weights=[2/3, 1/3]), cov index preserved in output index ordering. This is a gate-semantic redesign, not a calibration drift. Specifically: G0 plan=`universe_coverage_pct>=0.50` vs in-force=`oos_sharpe>=0.30` (metric redefinition: coverage vs signal magnitude — not comparable quantities); G1 plan=`ic_ir>=0.02` vs in-force=`permutation_p<0.05` (metric redefinition: effect size vs effect distinguishability from noise — IC_IR moved to G3 at 25x tighter threshold 0.50); G2 plan=`max_corr_with_existing<0.50` vs in-force=`ic_mean>=0.02` (metric redefinition + semantic swap: redundancy gate vs directional-strength gate — redundancy checking is ABSENT from the in-force gate family); G3 plan=`oos_sharpe_delta_vs_baseline>0.00` vs in-force=`ic_ir>=0.50` (metric redefinition: delta-Sharpe test against baseline vs standalone-IC-IR against absolute bar — delta-vs-baseline Sharpe test is ABSENT, re-appears partially in G5 as marginal_contribution IC-delta but not Sharpe-delta); G4 plan=`full_sample_sharpe_delta>0.00` vs in-force=`max_drawdown>=-0.30` (metric redefinition: OOS-vs-IS robustness consistency check vs drawdown-ceiling risk filter — full-sample robustness testing is ABSENT from the in-force gate family); G5 plan=`baseline_date_gap_days<=30` vs in-force=`marginal_contribution>0.00` (metric redefinition + semantic swap: data-hygiene stale-baseline gate vs ensemble-contribution delta-IC gate — data-hygiene gate is ABSENT). The redesign entered the repository at commit 339fa10 (a CI/CD infrastructure commit that did not mention gate redefinition), was never referenced in `docs/GOVERNANCE_LOG.md`, and was never cross-walked against the plan. GL-0002 through GL-0008 cite the plan's gate family in their Rationale/Criteria column while the underlying `results/factors/*/gate_results.json` evidence files were produced by the in-force gate family — a concrete reproducibility failure mode for the seven reject decisions. The audit identifies three clean correction paths (none implicitly endorsed by this iteration): (A) AMEND PLAN to match in-force config — write a new row in docs/GOVERNANCE_LOG.md freezing the in-force family and update /.claude/plans/dreamy-riding-quasar.md + docs/templates/factor_screen_memo.md §3 + GL-0002..GL-0008 to cite the actual gate family; retains work done since 2026-04-18; does NOT restore redundancy or data-hygiene gates; (B) AMEND CONFIG to match plan — rewrite config/gates.yaml to the plan's family (coverage / ic_ir / max_corr / sharpe_delta / fullsample_delta / date_gap), implement any missing metrics in src/nyse_core/gates.py, re-run the seven factor screens against corrected thresholds, issue a correction event for each reject decision; restores plan-of-record fidelity; discards current evidence; (C) HYBRID reconciliation — retain the in-force signal-quality gates (oos_sharpe, permutation_p, ic_ir, max_drawdown, marginal_contribution) AND add the plan's structurally missing gates (universe_coverage, max_corr_with_existing redundancy, data-hygiene date_gap) as additional pre-registered gates, expanding G0-G5 to G0-G8 (or equivalent re-indexing), freeze the extended family in governance log, re-run screens against the union. RECOMMENDED POSTURE for iter-11 operator decision: option (C) preserves the screening work done on the signal-quality side (no evidence discarded) while reinstating the redundancy and data-hygiene guards that the plan-of-record pre-registered. entry.summary: Wave-3 iter-10 supplemental ships `docs/audit/gate_mismatch_root_cause_and_consequences.md` (GCA-2026-04-23-supplemental) answering the operator's two follow-up questions from the iter-10 primary presentation: (Q1) why did plan and config mismatch, and (Q2) what happens if we continue using the in-force gates going forward. This memo is presentation-only, findings-only, changes no threshold/metric/direction/admission, and does NOT implicitly endorse any of the four correction paths offered in iter-10 primary. Q1 ROOT CAUSE: iter-1 code was authored off-repo between 2026-04-15 (initial commit 902cd41, plan+reference materials only, no src/) and 2026-04-18 (commit 339fa10, bulk source-tree landing). During that window the implementer rewrote the G0-G5 gate family against a signal-quality design axis rather than the plan's hygiene-gatekeeping design axis, ran all six factor screens (ivol_20d, high_52w, momentum_2_12, piotroski, accruals, profitability — all FAIL), wrote documentation describing the failures (commits ad9ab14, 588ffce), closed TODOs citing files not yet in git (commit 07d2bfa), and then bulk-committed the source tree under a commit-message framed as CI/CD infrastructure (commit 339fa10). Six contributing factors made the redesign invisible: (1) 339fa10 commit framing obscured the gate-family-defining nature of the change; (2) no analogous GOVERNANCE_LOG freeze row for gates.yaml (unlike GL-0001 for falsification_triggers.yaml); (3) config/gates.yaml line 5 smoking gun — 'These gates are aligned with factor_screening.screen_factor()' — config matches code, code does not match plan; (4) pre-commit hooks are syntactic not semantic; (5) GL-0002..GL-0008 rationale copied plan wording but evidence uses in-force verdicts; (6) prior researcher almost flagged at NYSE_ALPHA_RESEARCH_RECORD.md:373 but reframed as single-threshold typo rather than family-level redesign. Q2 CONSEQUENCES of continuing with in-force gates: FOUR STRUCTURAL GUARDS LOST — G0-plan universe-coverage (coverage-biased admission), G2-plan max-correlation (redundant admission of near-identical factors), G4-plan full-sample robustness (overfit passage when OOS Sharpe survives but IS Sharpe contradicts), G5-plan date-gap data-hygiene (stale-baseline comparison). FOUR SIGNAL-QUALITY BARS GAINED OR TIGHTENED — oos_sharpe ≥ 0.30 (absent from plan, added bar), permutation_p < 0.05 (absent from plan, added significance test), max_drawdown ≥ -0.30 (absent from plan, added tail-risk filter), ic_ir ≥ 0.50 (plan had 0.02 — 25x tighter). Severity ranking of lost guards: HIGH (redundancy — directly inflates ensemble IR estimates), MEDIUM-HIGH (full-sample robustness — classical anti-overfit), MEDIUM (universe coverage — material risk on fundamentals factors), LOW-MEDIUM (date-gap hygiene — procedural). Concrete failure modes unblocked: (a) redundant admission of momentum_6_12 alongside momentum_2_12 with Spearman IC correlation >0.5, double-counting signal; (b) coverage-biased admission of a factor active on 20% of universe early and 90% late, with attribution contamination; (c) overfit passage of a factor with OOS Sharpe 0.32 but IS Sharpe -0.15; (d) stale-baseline comparison against an 18-month-old ensemble baseline. NET ASSESSMENT: continuing with in-force is not catastrophic (signal-quality side is measurably stricter than plan in two places, with three added checks), but the loss of four hygiene guards is a structural weakening that should be explicit in the program's posture rather than silent. MITIGATION NOTE: even under path A, a standing TODO to re-evaluate redundancy at the ensemble-construction layer would bound the highest-severity consequence — this memo flags this forward-looking process note but does not implement it; iter-11 decides. iter-11 still requires an explicit operator `correction` authorization naming (1) chosen path A/B/C/D, (2) scope (files, whether screens re-run), (3) any supplementary mitigation. iter-11 does not proceed autonomously.
